meta_title: For Freelancers: user onboarding to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Onboarding UX Without Design Skills
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with UI. Use this intermediate guide to build high-converting user onboarding flows using Antigravity and Claude Code as a solo dev.
content.answer_block: You don't need a designer to build world-class onboarding. By leveraging component-based UI libraries and AI-driven code generation, you can implement high-converting, pattern-based onboarding flows. Use Antigravity to scaffold structured layouts and Claude Code to iterate on user flows, ensuring professional results that impress clients while saving you weeks of development time.
content.intro:
The blank canvas is a freelancer's greatest enemy. You have the logic, the database, and the backend, but your user onboarding looks like a wireframe from 2005. Clients don't care about your clean API; they care about the 'Aha!' moment within the first 60 seconds of interaction.

Stop agonizing over pixel-perfect margins. By shifting to a system-led design approach, you can bypass the 'no design skills' hurdle. We are going to deploy an automated, professional-grade onboarding sequence that converts users, all while keeping your development velocity at maximum speed.

steps.title: Define the Value Path
steps.description: Identify the one action that proves your app's value. We map this to a 3-step modal sequence.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React component for a 3-step onboarding carousel using Tailwind CSS. Include a progress indicator and a 'Skip' button. Ensure it is responsive and uses a clean, modern SaaS aesthetic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Analyze my current auth flow and suggest where to inject a 3-step onboarding modal that highlights core product features.'

steps.title: Scaffold UI Components
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate clean, accessible components that don't look like amateur hour.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable 'OnboardingStep' card component with an icon placeholder, a heading, and a description. Use a professional sans-serif font stack and soft drop shadows.

steps.title: Implement State Management
steps.description: Track user progress so they aren't bothered by onboarding after completion.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a React hook 'useOnboarding' that persists the user's progress to localStorage and provides methods to trigger, skip, or reset the onboarding flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply --file hooks/useOnboarding.ts --logic 'use localStorage to track completed steps and return a boolean isCompleted'

steps.title: Add Contextual Tooltips
steps.description: Guide users through complex UI elements without cluttering the screen.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a 'Tooltip' component that wraps an element and triggers on hover or click. Ensure it supports dynamic positioning using floating-ui.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Add a contextual tooltip to the main dashboard sidebar item that points to the new feature introduced in onboarding.'

steps.title: Final Polish and Integration
steps.description: Connect the flow to your app's lifecycle to ensure a seamless handoff.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate the onboarding flow into the App.tsx file. Ensure the modal only appears if the user is authenticated and hasn't seen the onboarding yet.

pro_tips: Always use a library like Shadcn/UI for components; it gives you the look of a premium designer without the need to write custom CSS.
Limit onboarding to 3 steps maximum. If you need more, you have a product complexity problem, not a design problem.
Use micro-animations to guide the eye toward the primary CTA; CSS transitions are enough to make a simple app feel 'pro'.
Track the drop-off rate of your onboarding steps using a simple console log event; if users leave at step 2, rewrite the copy immediately.
Never hardcode onboarding text. Move it to a JSON config file so you can A/B test the copy without redeploying the entire application.
content.next_steps: Once the onboarding flow is live, implement a 'Help' button that triggers a mini-version of this flow to serve as an evergreen resource for users.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting design gaps hold your SaaS back. Deploy professional onboarding today and watch your retention metrics climb. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
